posts.text:
US President Donald Trump announced a temporary pause of “Project Freedom,” a US naval initiative aimed at ensuring navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, citing progress toward a potential agreement with Iran.

“Based on the request of Pakistan and other Countries, the tremendous Military Success that we have had during the Campaign against the Country of Iran and, additionally, the fact that Great Progress has been made toward a Complete and Final Agreement with Representatives of Iran, we have mutually agreed that, while the Blockade will remain in full force and effect, Project Freedom (The Movement of Ships through the Strait of Hormuz) will be paused for a short period of time to see whether or not the Agreement can be finalized and signed,” Trump wrote on Truth Social.

The announcement follows a brief and intense phase of US military activity tied to the initiative, which lasted roughly 48 hours before being halted. The operation had been presented by Washington as a step to secure maritime routes amid escalating tensions with Iran, despite it being yet another inflammatory move by the Trump administration.

According to sources cited by Al Mayadeen, no US or other vessels have successfully passed through the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, despite US claims of resumed maritime transit.

Israeli media reported on behind-the-scenes coordination between the United States and "Israel", following an unusual meeting between #US Ambassador to "Israel" Mike Huckabee and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in occupied al-Quds.

According to Israeli Kan news, the United Arab Emirates is no longer satisfied with condemnations and is exerting pressure on the United States to strike #Iran, arguing that Tehran should be "punished" over the alleged missile launches, although Iran has denied them. 

In more detail, "Israel’s" ambassador to the #UAE, Yossi Shelley, spoke with a senior official in Abu Dhabi, who told him that they must "make the American administration act," adding that "it is impossible to be tolerant toward the Iranian regime in any form; they must be punished."

The United States, however, has maintained ambiguity over the content of the discussions, stating, "As a matter of policy, we do not comment on private diplomatic conversations."

#IranWar #WarOnIran

A group of House Democrats is calling on the Trump administration to openly recognize "Israel’s" undeclared nuclear weapons program.

In a letter to Marco Rubio obtained by The Washington Post, more than two dozen lawmakers led by Joaquin Castro argue that Washington’s continued silence over the Israeli nuclear program is unjustifiable, particularly amid the war in Iran and the heightened risk of military escalation.

“The risks of miscalculation, escalation, and nuclear use in this environment are not theoretical,” the lawmakers wrote. “Congress has a constitutional responsibility to be fully informed about the nuclear balance in the Middle East, the risk of escalation by any party to this conflict, and the administration’s planning and contingencies for such scenarios.”

Successive American administrations have never addressed "Israel's" undeclared nuclear program since the 1960s, with the exception of John F. Kennedy, who strongly opposed the program and reportedly demanded inspections at the Dimona facility.

"Israel", " which is not an NPT signatory, maintains a policy of nuclear ambiguity regarding its nuclear weapons, neither affirming nor denying it has them. But it is widely believed to be in possession of dozens of nuclear warheads.

#UnitedStates #NuclearProgram #Trump #AlMayadeen
